title: What is Dubly.AI?
content:
What is Dubly.AI?
Copy
Dubly.AI is an AI-powered video localization platform that lets you translate, dub and lip-sync videos into more than 30 languages — without re-recording audio, hiring voice actors, or opening a traditional editor.
What Dubly.AI does for you
AI Transcription
– Converts the spoken audio in your source video into an editable transcript.
AI Translation
– Translates your transcript into any supported target language, context- and brand-aware.
AI Voice & Voice Cloning
– Generates a natural-sounding dub with a studio voice or a clone of the original speaker.
Lip-Sync
– Aligns the translated audio to the speaker's mouth movements so the result feels native, not dubbed.
Review & Export
– Review, edit, approve, and export a ready-to-publish file.
Who Dubly.AI is for
Dubly.AI is built for teams that publish video at scale:
Marketing and brand teams localizing campaigns
E-learning and corporate training departments
Creators expanding to international audiences
Agencies producing multilingual video for clients
Why teams choose Dubly.AI
Turn one video into 30+ localized versions in a single workflow
Cut localization cost and turnaround time by up to 90% vs. traditional dubbing studios
Preserve brand voice across markets with glossaries and voice cloning
Keep everything in one place: transcription, translation, dubbing, review, and export
Next step:
Read the
Quick-Start Guide
to produce your first localized video in under 10 minutes.

title: Create an account and log in
content:
Create an account and log in
Copy
This article walks you through signing up for Dubly.AI, verifying your email, and what to expect on your first login.
Creating your account
Go to
dubly.ai
and click
Login
→
Sign Up
.
Fill in the signup form:
First name
and
last name
— shown to teammates later.
Email
— this will be your login. Use an address you check, because you'll need to verify it.
Company name
— used to name your organization inside Dubly.AI. You'll see it in the sidebar; your teammates see it when you invite them.
Password
— at least 8 characters, with at least one lowercase letter, one uppercase letter, and one digit.
Click
Sign Up
. By submitting, you accept the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy (both linked next to the button).
Verifying your email
After you submit:
Check your inbox for a verification email from Dubly.AI.
Click the link in the email.
You land on the
Dashboard
with a confirmation message.
If the email doesn't arrive within a few minutes, check your spam folder. Still nothing? Email
hello@dubly.ai
from the address you tried to sign up with.
What gets created automatically
When you verify your email, Dubly.AI sets up:
Your personal organization
, named after the company name you entered at signup.
1 free credit
— enough to try a short dub end-to-end before you subscribe.
Owner role
for you in the new organization.
There's no separate onboarding questionnaire — you land straight on the Dashboard ready to start.
Joining an existing organization via invite
If someone invited you to their Dubly.AI organization,
don't sign up from the homepage
. Instead:
Open the invitation email.
Click the link — it takes you to a signup form with your email already filled in.
Set your first name, last name, and password. That's it — no company name, because you're joining an existing organization rather than creating a new one.
For more on how invites work, see
Inviting Team Members and Managing Roles
.
Logging in
Log in at
app.dubly.ai
with your email and password. You land on the
Dashboard
— a quick-access view of your recent dubs, your plan status, and shortcuts to start a new project or change settings.
Sign-in is currently
email and password only
. The Google sign-in button on the login page is disabled while we finish migrating that flow.
Forgot your password?
On the login page, click
Forgot password?
Enter your email. A reset link is sent to your inbox.
Follow the link and set a new password.
The new password must meet the same rules as at signup (8+ characters, at least one lowercase letter, one uppercase letter, and one digit).
Common issues
Email already registered
— use the forgot-password flow to recover access.
Verification link expired
— request a new verification email from the login screen.
Still stuck
— contact
hello@dubly.ai
from any email you can reach, and include the email address on the account.

title: Quick-start guide: Your first dub
content:
Quick-start guide: Your first dub
Copy
This guide walks you through your first localized video in Dubly.AI — from sign-in to download — in under 10 minutes.
1. Sign in
Log in at
app.dubly.ai
. If you don't have an account yet, sign up — your first minute is free as part of the free trial.
2. Start a new dub
From the Dashboard, click
+ Create Dub
in the top right corner. The Create Dub modal opens.
If you want to organize related dubs into folders, create a
Project
first from the Dubs page and start your dub from inside the project.
3. Add your source video
The modal opens on the source step with two tabs:
YouTube Link
— paste a standard YouTube URL. YT-Shorts and
youtu.be
short links are not supported — convert them to the standard format first.
Upload Videofile
— drag and drop, or click to browse.
MP4 or MOV, up to 5 GB
.
Click
Continue
when the video is ready.
4. Configure the dub
The next step has three tabs:
Details
,
Translation Styles
, and
Advanced
.
Details tab
Dub name
— appears in your dub overview later.
Source language
— pick the language spoken in the video. We support
99 source languages
. Auto-detect usually works, but setting it manually is more reliable for short or bilingual videos.
Target languages
— pick one or more languages you want to dub into. Many languages also offer dialects (for example: Spanish (Latin America) vs. Spanish (Spain), English (US) vs. English (UK)).
Voice cloning style
— choose one of:
Original (Recommended)
— clones the speakers' voices, keeps the natural feel.
Studio-Like
— cleaner and more polished, less authentic.
Replace Voice
— swaps the speaker for a voice from our library. After processing starts you'll be able to assign a library voice per speaker.
Number of speakers
—
1
,
2
, or
Autodetect (3 or more)
. Picking the correct number gives better diarization than autodetect when you know it.
Custom Voice
(if enabled on your plan)
— assign one specific voice to all speakers across all target languages.
Translation Styles tab
For each target language, optionally pick a
Translation Style
— a reusable bundle of don't-translate words, custom translations, pronunciations, tone, formality, and writing style. You can also create a new style directly from this tab. See
Translation Styles
for setup details.
Advanced tab
Watermark
— upload a JPEG or PNG (max 5 MB), pick a corner, and set the duration (permanent or 5/10/20/30/60/120 seconds).
Keep original voice in background
(only with Replace Voice)
— mix the original speaker's voice quietly under the new voice, with an adjustable volume slider.
5. Confirm and start
At the bottom of the modal:
Tick the consent checkbox confirming you have the rights to translate the video and clone the voices.
Check the cost — you'll see how many minutes the dub will use and how many you have left.
Click
Create Dub
to start processing.
You'll be redirected to your dubs list. Processing usually takes a few minutes — you can leave the page and come back later.
6. Review and edit
Once the dub is finished, open it from your
My Dubs
list. You'll see a video preview and tabs for
Edit Translation
,
Lip Sync
, and
Download
.
Use the
Advanced Editor
in the Edit Translation tab to:
Fix transcription mistakes by editing the source text — the translation regenerates automatically.
Rewrite specific lines by editing the target text directly — the voice is re-synthesized on your exact wording.
Reassign speakers if the diarizer got one wrong.
After your edits, click
Update
to render the updated dub.
7. Add Lip-Sync (optional)
For videos with an on-camera speaker, switch to the
Lip Sync
tab and request lip sync. This re-aligns the mouth movements to the dubbed audio. Lip sync uses additional minutes — check the cost shown before you confirm.
8. Download
Open the
Download
tab to grab:
The dubbed video (MP4)
The dubbed audio (MP3) — full mix, voice only, or background only
An SRT subtitle file
The lip-synced video (if you've requested lip sync)
That's it — your first localized video is ready to ship.

title: Dashboard overview: Where to find everything
content:
Dashboard overview: Where to find everything
Copy
When you log in to Dubly.AI you land on the
Dashboard
. Here's a tour of the main areas.
Left sidebar
The left sidebar is your main navigation and is visible on every page after you log in.
Organization Switcher
At the top of the sidebar, you'll see the name of your current organization (only if you belong to more than one). Click it to switch between organizations you're a member of, or to create a new organization.
Navigation
Home
– Return to the dashboard at any time.
My Dubs
– View and manage all your dubs.
My Plan
– See your plan details, remaining minutes, transactions, and invoices.
Members
(not available on free plans) – Invite and manage team members on your account.
Settings
– Manage your account, API tokens, and custom voices.
Translation Style
– Configure how your translations sound.
Help Center
– Articles, tutorials, and contact support.
Minutes Widget
Down on the left, the
Minutes
widget shows a progress bar with how much of your monthly allowance is still available. Click
More Minutes
to jump straight to the plan page and top up.
If you don't have an active plan yet, this area shows a
Get Started
card with a
Start Plan
button instead.
Profile & Logout
At the very bottom of the sidebar, you'll find your avatar, name, and email address. Click the logout icon next to your name to sign out.
Collapse / Expand
Use the panel icon next to the Dubly logo to collapse the sidebar into a compact icon-only view, or expand it back to full width. On mobile, the sidebar is hidden by default — open it with the menu button in the top bar.
Top Right Corner Options
In the top right corner of every page, you'll find two controls:
Language Selector (🌐 EN)
— Click this to change the display language of the interface. This only affects the platform UI, not the language of your actual dub projects.
Theme Toggle (☀️)
— Switch between light and dark mode by clicking the sun/moon icon.
What you'll find on the Home page
When you log in, the Home page greets you with a personalized welcome message and gives you instant access to everything you need to manage your dubs and account.
Create Dub button
In the top right corner, you'll find the
+ Create Dub
button. Click it anytime you want to start a new translation project.
Quick access cards
The Home page features four quick-access cards that take you to the most important areas of your account:
My Dubs
— Total number of dubs you've created. Click to see all previous dubs in one place; watch, download, or edit them.
Settings
— Account settings area: personal details, password, API tokens, custom voices.
Find out about plans
— Overview of your current plan, remaining credits and subscription details.
Help Center
— Opens the Help Center in a new tab.
My Dubs
Below the quick-access cards, the
My Dubs
section shows your most recently created translation projects so you can jump back into your work without navigating to the full dubs list.
Filter by status
: All Dubs / Finished / In Progress / Updating.
Search
for a specific dub by title.
View toggle
: switch between grid and list view on desktop.
See all
next to the section title to open the full My Dubs overview.
My Plan overview
The
My Plan
page gives you a complete overview of your current subscription and everything you need to manage your plan, track your usage, and keep an eye on your billing.
Navigation tabs:
Plan
— All details about your active subscription. Default view when you have an active plan; disabled until you start one if you don't.
Get More Minutes
— Pricing calculator for starting a new plan or topping up.
Transactions
— Complete history of past transactions and credit movements.
Invoices
— Access and download invoices (only shown if you have invoices).
Your plan details
Remaining Minutes
— How many minutes you have left this month, with a progress bar.
Plan
— The name of your active subscription (e.g. "125 Minutes – Monthly Plan").
Status
— Active, Cancelled, or Inactive.
Cost
— Per month or per year depending on your billing interval.
Started
— The date your subscription started.
Next Billing
(or
Ends on
for cancelled plans) — Renewal date, or when access ends if cancelled.
Payment method
— Card type and last four digits. Click
Edit
to update.
Manage Subscription button
At the bottom of the plan details, click
Manage Subscription
to upgrade, downgrade, or change your plan via the customer portal. If cancelled, you'll see
Reactivate Subscription
instead.
My invoices shortcut
On the right side of the page, the
My invoices
quick-access card jumps you directly to an overview of all your invoices.

title: Key dubbing terms explained
content:
Key dubbing terms explained
Copy
A quick reference for the terminology used across Dubly.AI and this Help Center.
Term
Definition
AI Dubbing
The automated process of replacing the original audio of a video with a localized AI-generated version, preserving the original tone and emotion.
ASR (Automatic Speech Recognition)
The technology that turns spoken audio into text. Dubly.AI uses ASR to create your transcript.
Dialect
A regional variant of a language, e.g., Spanish Peninsular vs. Spanish Latin American.
Voice Cloning
A sophisticated technology that analyzes the unique characteristics of an original voice and replicates them, allowing the translation to sound like the original speaker.
Lip-Sync (Visual Alignment)
The AI-driven modification of the speaker's mouth movements in the video to match the phonetic sounds of the target language.
Speech-to-Text (STT)
The underlying process of transcribing the spoken audio from the source file into a written script for translation.
Source Language
The language originally spoken in the uploaded video. Dubly.AI uses auto-detection, but this can be manually adjusted for better accuracy.
Target Language
The destination language into which you want your content translated and dubbed.
Dub
A "Dub" refers to the final synchronized audio track in the target language. Unlike a simple voiceover, a dub aims to match the timing, tone, and performance of the original speaker, creating a seamless viewing experience in a new language.
Advanced Editor
The professional-grade workspace within Dubly.AI designed for high-precision adjustments. The Advanced Editor allows users to:
• Manually adjust
Phonetic Spelling
to fix AI pronunciation errors.
• Control
Speaker Assignment
across complex scenes with multiple participants.
• Preview specific segments before the final render.
SRT File (SubRip Subtitle)
A widely compatible, plain-text file format used for subtitles. An SRT file exported from Dubly.AI contains:
1. The sequential number of the subtitle.
2. The precise Start and End Time (Time-codes).
3. The translated text.
Organization
The container for your projects, team members, billing plan, and Translation Styles. Every Dubly.AI account belongs to at least one organization, created automatically at signup. A single login can belong to multiple organizations.
Organization Switcher
The dropdown at the top of the sidebar for jumping between organizations you belong to. Only visible if multi-organization access is enabled on your account.
Roles (Owner, Admin, Member, Native Speaker)
The four permission levels inside an organization. Owner has full control including billing (one per organization). Admin manages Members and Native Speakers. Member works on projects but cannot manage the team. Native Speaker is a review-focused role for teammates quality-checking dubs in their native language.
Subdub
A per-target-language version of a Dub. One Dub with three target languages contains three subdubs, one per language. Each subdub can have its own Translation Style, voice assignment, and lip-sync status.
Translation Style
A reusable bundle of translation rules for a specific source–target language pair. Includes a do-not-translate list, custom word pairs, pronunciation hints, writing style, tone, and formality. Applied per target language when creating a Dub. Used to keep brand terminology consistent.
Do-Not-Translate List
A section of a Translation Style listing words that must stay in the source language. Typical use: brand names, product names, trademarks, URLs, SKUs.
Custom Voice
A voice pre-configured for your organization that can be selected as the speaking voice for a Dub, overriding the automatic voice clone. Stored with an ElevenLabs
Voice ID
.
Voice ID
The unique identifier for a voice in our voice-synthesis provider (ElevenLabs). Used internally to tell the TTS system which voice to use for a given speaker. Every Custom Voice and every automatically generated voice clone has one.
Speaker Assignment
The step in the dub workflow where you confirm which cloned voice belongs to which on-screen speaker. Triggered automatically when Dubly.AI detects more than one speaker in your source video. The Dub pauses in the "Assign Voices" state until you complete this step.
Subscription Minutes
The minutes (credits) included in your monthly or yearly plan. Reset at the start of every billing cycle — unused minutes do not roll over. Always spent before any one-time-purchase minutes.
One-Time-Purchase Minutes (OTP)
Minutes bought as a one-off top-up outside your subscription. Never expire. Used automatically once your subscription minutes run out in a cycle.
API Token
A credential for programmatic access to Dubly.AI via the public API. Tied to a specific organization; anything created with the token (dubs, credit usage) happens in that organization. Created and revoked from
Settings → API
.

title: Contact support
content:
Contact support
Copy
Need help? Here are all the ways to reach the Dubly.AI team — and what to send so we can resolve your question fast.
Pick the right channel
Channel
Use for
Response Time
In-app chat
Quick how-tos, account issues, and immediate guidance.
< 2 hours
(during business hours)
Email
(
hello@dubly.ai
)
General support inquiries, detailed project problems, or feedback.
< 24 hours
AI Chat Bot
Finding instant answers within our Help Center articles.
Instant
How to reach us in-app
Click the message icon in the bottom-right of the app.
Choose
the category of your issue.
Define your issue via text.
What to include in a support request
The more context we have, the faster we can help:
Dub link
(copy from the browser address bar)
Account email
What you expected
to happen
What actually happened
, including any
error message
(screenshots welcome)
Enterprise support
Enterprise customers have:
Slack Connect
channel for day-to-day communication
Guaranteed SLAs
for response and resolution time
24/7 on-call
for sev-1 incidents

for details.

Account email:

What you expected
to happen.

title: Free trial: Translate your first video at no cost
content:
Free trial: Translate your first video at no cost
Copy
Want to see what Dubly.AI can do before you commit? Every new account gets
1 free credit
at signup — enough to translate roughly one minute of video, end to end, with all features unlocked. No credit card, no time limit.
What's included in the free credit
The free credit is
not a stripped-down preview
. It gives you full access to:
Translation
into any of our supported languages and dialects.
Voice cloning
of the original speaker — included, no extra credit cost.
Lip-sync
— your first lip-sync run per dub is also free, so you can test it on the same video.
Multi-speaker detection
if your video has more than one speaker on camera.
All export formats
— MP4, MP3, voice-only audio track, and SRT subtitles.
The goal is that what you test is exactly what you'd get on a paid plan.
How to redeem it
Sign up for a Dubly.AI account at
dubly.ai
. No credit card required.
Verify your email.
Open
Create Dub
and upload a short video — anything under one minute is ideal.
Choose your target language. The cost preview will show
1 credit
, and your balance shows the free credit ready to spend.
Click create. Processing takes 3–5 minutes for most videos. See
Processing takes too long
if it takes longer.
You can preview the result in the browser before downloading. Lip-sync can be turned on after the first dub finishes — see
When should you turn on lip-sync?
Tips for a representative test
The free credit is short. To get a result that actually predicts how Dubly will perform on your real content:
Use a clip from your real workflow
, not a stock video. Your speaker, your audio setup, your background music.
Turn on lip-sync
if your real videos have an on-camera speaker. The free run lets you test it.
Read
Do's and don'ts for your source video
before you upload — five minutes of prep makes the test more realistic.
After the free credit
Subscribe
to a monthly or yearly plan. Plans include a fixed number of minutes per cycle and unlock multi-user workspaces and priority processing. See
Billing and pricing overview
for the current options.
Users invited into an existing organization don't get a separate free credit — they share the organization's balance.
Common questions
Can I get more than one free credit?
The free credit is one-time per account. If you want to test more before subscribing,
book a call
— we're happy to walk through your specific use case live and might be able to offer more free credits.
Does the free credit expire?
No. It sits on your balance until you use it.
Can I use the dubbed video commercially?
Free credits are intended for testing and evaluation purposes only. While you generally own your output, commercial usage is reserved for paid plans. To use your dubbed content for business or monetization, simply switch to a paid plan.
What if my test fails?
Failed dubs are reviewed manually and credits restored where the failure was on our side. Email
hello@dubly.ai
with the dub title and what went wrong. See
Refunds and credit recovery
for the details.
Is it allowed to use multiple accounts for testing?
To keep things fair, we limit free credits to one account per user. Creating multiple accounts to bypass these limits is against our terms. However, if our standard trial isn't enough to evaluate Dubly for your specific business case, please contact
Sales
or
schedule a demo
. We'd love to find a solution for your larger-scale projects.

title: How can I transfer the ownership of my Workspace?
content:
How can I transfer the ownership of my Workspace?
Copy
Transferring ownership of your Account to another person isn't a self-service action right now. The product blocks owner-level changes through the Members page on purpose — owner status controls billing and the right to delete the Account, so we treat changes carefully. This article walks through the process.
Step 1 — Get the new owner into the Account first
The new owner must already be a Member of your Account before the transfer. If they're not yet:
Open the
Members
page.
Click
Invite Member
, enter their email, role doesn't matter (Admin or Member is fine — it'll be overwritten).
Wait until they accept the invitation and complete signup. Don't skip this step. Without an existing Dubly.AI user account for the new owner, the transfer can't go through.
Step 2 — Contact support
Email support from the
current owner's
email address. Include:
Your Account name (so we can find the right one)
The email address of the new owner (must match the Member you invited in Step 1)
A short note confirming you're authorizing the transfer We verify both sides:
That the request actually comes from the current owner (we check the sender email).
That the new owner is a Member of the Account. For higher-stakes transfers (large account, enterprise contract), we may ask for additional confirmation from the new owner directly before proceeding.
Step 3 — We complete the transfer
We swap the roles in the database:
Old owner → demoted to Admin (still has full functional access, just not billing).
New owner → promoted to Owner (gains billing access, can delete the Account, can manage all members). This happens in a single atomic operation, so the Account is never without an owner.
What changes after the transfer
Billing emails
start going to the new owner.
The new owner
is the only person who can delete the Account or change other Owner-level settings.
Subscription, dubs, projects, and all other data
stay exactly where they are.
What if the current owner has lost access?
If the current owner's email is dead (left the company, defunct domain), it's harder. Contact support and we'll work through additional verification — usually proof of payment from the original card or an email from a verifiable @companydomain address.
Why isn't this a button?
Owner transfers are high-stakes and infrequent — most accounts go years without one. We've prioritized other features over building a self-service transfer flow. If you'd find it valuable, let us know — feature priorities are partly driven by what customers ask for.

title: How to remove a member from your account
content:
How to remove a member from your account
Copy
When someone leaves your team — they switch jobs, you wrap up a project, or you simply no longer need them in the Account — you can remove them. Only Owners and Admins can remove members.
What removal does
Removing a member:
Revokes their access immediately.
They can no longer log into your Account.
Doesn't delete their work.
Dubs they created stay in the Account.
Doesn't delete their user account.
They can still log into Dubly.AI itself with other Accounts they belong to. Removal is a hard, immediate action. There's no "deactivated" state where they keep partial access.
How to remove someone
Open the
Members
page from your Account settings.
Find the person you want to remove.
Open their row's actions menu and choose
Remove
.
Confirm. That's it — they're out as soon as you confirm.
Who you can't remove
The Owner.
The Owner role can't be removed; ownership has to be transferred to someone else first.
Yourself, if you're the Owner.
Same reason.
Before you remove someone
For an organized hand-off:
Make sure their active dubs aren't a surprise to anyone.
Dubs they started will continue, but if you want to track them, note them down before removing.
Document any custom voices, glossaries, or Translation Styles they configured
so the rest of the team knows where they came from. For a more thorough hand-off, see
How to off-board a member
.

title: How to off-board a member
content:
How to off-board a member
Copy
Off-boarding someone from your Dubly.AI Account is more than just clicking "remove". You want to make sure their work stays intact, their access is fully revoked, and the rest of your team isn't surprised by anything missing afterward. This is a checklist for a clean off-board.
Before removing the member
1. Reassign work in progress
If they have Native Speaker assignments open, reassign them now. Native Speaker assignments don't auto-transfer when the person is removed, and you'll lose track of them. If they've been editing transcriptions or translations on a dub, make sure their changes are saved.
2. Document what they own
Walk through anything specific they configured:
Custom voices
they created or set up.
Translation Styles
or
Glossaries
they tuned.
Projects
they own.
Pinned or active dubs
they were running. Capture enough that someone else can pick up the work.
3. Track ownership where it matters
Dubly.AI doesn't formally transfer dub ownership when you remove a member — the dub stays in the Account regardless. But if your team tracks "who owns this dub" externally (in a project manager, a spreadsheet), now's the time to update those records.
4. Check shared external access
If the member shared Dubly.AI dubs with people outside your Account (via a share link), those external links keep working. If you want to revoke an external share, do it on the dub itself, not by removing the member.
Removing the member
Once everything above is handled, remove them as described in
How to remove a member from your account
.
After removing them
5. Verify their access is gone
If you have any way to test (a shared device, a screen recording from their last session), confirm they can no longer log into the Account.
6. Communicate to the team
Let the team know who's been removed and what's been reassigned. People referencing "ask Sara" don't appreciate finding out Sara is gone after the fact.
7. Cancel pending invites if needed
If the member had open invitations to other people that hadn't been accepted yet, those invites still work. Review pending invites and revoke any that no longer apply.
What if they come back
If someone you removed needs access again, just invite them again. The history on the dubs they touched stays intact, but their new membership starts fresh.

title: What happens to a member's content when their account is deleted?
content:
What happens to a member's content when their account is deleted?
Copy
This is a question about what happens to your work — dubs, projects, custom voices — when you or a teammate stops being part of an Account, or stops having a Dubly.AI user account at all. The short answer:
the work stays.
What changes is who has access to it.
When a member is removed from your Account
If you remove someone from your Account (or they leave), nothing they created disappears:
Dubs they made
stay in the Account. Anyone with the right role can still open, edit, share, or delete them.
Projects they organized
stay where they were.
Custom voices, glossaries, and Translation Styles
they configured remain available to the rest of the team.
Native Speaker assignments
to them are released. The dub still exists, but no one is currently assigned to review it. Reassign as needed. What's lost is
their access
— they can't log into your Account anymore — but not their work.
When a Dubly.AI user deletes their entire user account
If a person deletes their Dubly.AI user account (not just leaves your Account), the same is mostly true:
Dubs they created
stay in any Accounts they were a member of.
Their authorship attribution
is removed — the dubs are still there, but they no longer show "created by [name]" pointing at a specific user.
They are removed as a member
from every Account they belonged to. The Account doesn't lose the work; it loses the person.
Why work stays in the Account
A Dubly.AI Account is a shared resource — its dubs, projects, and settings belong to the Account, not to whichever individual created them. This is intentional: it means a single person leaving doesn't take their work with them, and the rest of the team isn't blocked.
What if the dubs really should go
If your team has a policy that a removed member's dubs should be deleted (e.g. for confidentiality reasons), an Owner or Admin needs to delete them manually after the member is removed. There's no automatic cleanup.

title: Invite reviewers to check your translations
content:
Invite reviewers to check your translations
Copy
Share link with PIN
Use a Share link when the reviewer just needs to
watch
the dub and tell you what they think. Setup:
From your dub, generate a share link. Dubly.AI gives you a URL with a 4-digit PIN.
Send the URL and PIN to the reviewer.
They open the link, enter the PIN and watch every language version of the dub.
Reviewers don't need a Dubly.AI account. The PIN keeps the link from being usable by random visitors. What a Share link reviewer can do:
Watch every language version of the dub.
Switch between languages from a selector in the player. What they can't do:
Edit translations.
Comment inside Dubly.AI.
See your other dubs or team.
Share just one language version (the link includes all languages — see
Share a dub
).
Invite as a workspace Member
Use a workspace
Member
invite when the reviewer should actually
edit
translations themselves — fix wording, correct names, polish tone — not just give you written feedback to action afterwards. Setup:
Open the
Members
page in your workspace settings.
Click
Invite Member
, enter the reviewer's email and pick the
Member
role.
They receive an email invite. Once they accept (creating a Dubly.AI account, or signing into an existing one), they can open any dub in the workspace and edit it directly in the
Edit Translation
tab. What a Member can do:
Open dubs and edit translations, source text, or speaker assignments — see
Editing a segment: original text and translation
Trigger a re-render via the
Update
button to apply their edits — see
Fine-tune your dub with the Advanced Editor
Create new dubs and use credits from the workspace What a Member can't do:
Invite other people to the workspace
Change workspace settings or billing
Member access is workspace-wide. A reviewer invited as a Member will see and can edit every dub in your workspace, not just the one you'd like reviewed. If that's a concern, stick with the Share link with PIN above (view-only).
For the full role comparison, see
Workspaces and user roles
.

title: Invite team members and manage their roles
content:
Invite team members and manage their roles
Copy
Bringing the right people into your Dubly.AI organization is how you scale from solo work to a real team. This article covers how invites work, what each role can do, and how to manage pending invites and existing members.
Is team management available on your plan?
Team invites are available on paid plans. Open the sidebar — if you see
Members
, you're set. If you don't, team features aren't enabled on your current plan. Contact
hello@dubly.ai
to upgrade.
Who can invite whom?
Each organization has exactly
one Owner
— ownership is transferred, not shared. You cannot invite a second Owner.
Owner
can invite Admins, Members, and Native Speakers.
Admin
can invite Members and Native Speakers.
Member
cannot invite anyone.
How to send an invite
Open
Members
from the sidebar.
Click
Invite Member
.
Enter one or more email addresses — comma-separated for bulk invites.
Pick a role from the dropdown.
Click
Send Invites
.
An invitation email goes out with a secure link that expires after
7 days
.
What each role does
Role
Team management
Billing
Org settings
Owner
Full — invites, removes, and changes any non-owner role
Yes
Yes
Admin
Manages Members and Native Speakers
No
No
Member
None
No
No
Rule of thumb:
give the lowest role that still lets the person do their job. You can change it later.
What the invited person sees
When your teammate opens the email and clicks the link, one of three things happens:
No Dubly.AI account yet
— a signup form appears with the email pre-filled. They set a name and password, and the invite is accepted automatically.
Already has an account with that email
— a confirmation dialog shows the organization name and their role. One click and they're in.
Logged in with a different email
— they'll see an error. They need to sign out and open the invite link from the inbox of the invited email address.
Managing pending invites
Pending invites are listed separately from active members on the Members page. For each one you can:
Resend
— generates a new link and sends a fresh email. Useful if the first one expired or got lost.
Revoke
— cancels the invite immediately. The link stops working.
You can select multiple invites and resend or revoke them in bulk.
Changing roles and removing members
From the Members page, open the action menu next to a member:
Change role
— Owners can change any non-Owner role. Admins can only change Members and Native Speakers.
Remove
— revokes access immediately. Owners can remove any non-Owner; Admins can only remove Members and Native Speakers.
The Owner's role cannot be changed from the UI, and the Owner cannot be removed.
Leaving an organization
If you want to step out of an organization:
As an Admin or Member:
ask an Owner or Admin to remove you from the Members page.
As the Owner:
you can't leave on your own — you'd leave the organization without an Owner. Contact
hello@dubly.ai
and we'll either transfer ownership to someone you designate or close the organization with you.
Still need help?
For anything the flows above don't cover contact
hello@dubly.ai
from the email on your account.

title: Organizations and user roles
content:
Organizations and user roles
Copy
An
organization
in Dubly.AI is the container for your projects, team, and billing. When you sign up, one is created for you automatically. This article covers what an organization is, how to switch between multiple organizations, and a quick reference for roles. (For the detailed invite flow and role permissions, see
Inviting Team Members and Managing Roles
.)
Your personal organization
Every Dubly.AI account gets a personal organization at signup, named after the company name you entered. It holds:
All your
projects
(dubs, translations, lip-syncs).
Your
team members
and their roles.
Your
billing plan
and credit balance.
Your
Translation Styles
(brand glossary, pronunciation, tone).
Anything you create or buy lives inside this organization — not on your personal profile.
Belonging to multiple organizations
A single Dubly.AI login can belong to
any number of organizations
. Each organization has its own projects, billing, and team — but you manage them all from one login.
When you belong to more than one organization, an
Organization switcher
appears at the top of the sidebar. Click it to jump between them. Projects, credits, team — everything you see switches with it.
Don't see a switcher?
Multi-organization access is enabled per organization. If you need it, contact
hello@dubly.ai
and we'll turn it on for your account.
Creating an additional organization
With multi-organization access enabled, open the switcher and click
Create New Organization
. You'll be asked for:
Organization name
That's it. A new, empty organization is created and you're its Owner.
Note: the free signup credit applies only to your
first
(personal) organization. New organizations you create later start with zero credits until you subscribe or buy minutes.
Renaming, deleting, or leaving an organization
These aren't yet available as self-service actions:
Renaming an organization
— contact
hello@dubly.ai
with the current and new name.
Deleting an organization
— contact support. Deletion is permanent and removes all projects, exports, members, and Translation Styles.
Leaving an organization
as a non-Owner — ask an Owner or Admin of that organization to remove you from the
Members
page. If the Owner is unreachable, contact support.
Leaving as the Owner
— not possible; you'd leave the organization without an Owner. Contact support to transfer ownership or close the organization.
Role summary
Each organization has four role types:
Role
What they do
Owner
Full control — projects, billing, members, organization settings. One Owner per organization.
Admin
Everything except billing and organization deletion. Can manage Members and Native Speakers.
Member
Works on projects. Cannot manage the team.
For the full matrix of who can invite, change, or remove whom, see
Inviting Team Members and Managing Roles
.
When to use multiple organizations
Multiple organizations make sense when you need
hard separation
between contexts:
An agency keeping each client's projects, members, and billing separate.
A parent company managing several brands with their own budgets.
A freelancer belonging to their personal organization plus each client's.
Most teams only ever need one organization — invite your colleagues into it rather than spinning up new organizations for every project.

title: When should you turn on lip-sync?
content:
When should you turn on lip-sync?
Copy
Lip-Sync is optional and costs extra. It's powerful when the video actually benefits from it, and a waste of credits when it doesn't. This article is a quick decision framework.
Enable Lip-Sync when:
The speaker is
on camera most of the time
and facing the audience.
The
face is prominent in the frame
— talking-head videos, interviews, product demos with a presenter, ads.
The
mouth is clearly visible
throughout.
You're publishing to audiences who expect a localized look (social, YouTube, ads, branded content). For these, Lip-Sync adds the visual polish that separates a dubbed video from a voice-over.
Skip Lip-Sync when:
The speaker is
rarely on camera
— voice-over videos, tutorials with screen recording, product videos with minimal presenter footage.
The video is
mostly B-roll, animation, or motion graphics
with no human face.
Shots are very
wide
or the face is too small to benefit.
The face is
frequently occluded
(close microphones, masks, heavy beards).
You're shipping
internal content
where the extra polish isn't worth the credit cost. For these, ship just the dubbed audio with the original video — it looks more natural than a forced Lip-Sync and saves you credits.
How to turn it on
Open your finished dub.
Open the
Lip Sync
tab on the dub detail page.
Click
Start Lip Sync
. You'll see the credit cost before you commit.
Wait for processing to finish — Lip-Sync runs independently of the main dub and usually takes a few minutes per segment. Lip-Sync is decided per subdub (per target language), so you can turn it on for the languages where it matters most and skip it elsewhere.
Cost
1 credit per minute
per subdub, on top of the dubbing cost. A 10-minute video dubbed into 3 languages with Lip-Sync for all three: 30 credits dubbing + 30 credits Lip-Sync =
60 credits total
.
Free trial:
Your first Lip-Sync per subdub is free — enough to test the feature on a short clip before you commit on longer content.
Changed your mind after running it?
You can always download the dubbed-audio version without Lip-Sync — both are available on the dub detail page once processing finishes. Lip-Sync credits aren't refunded automatically if you decide not to use the result, so use the free-trial to preview before running it on longer content.

title: Filming tips for perfect lip-sync
content:
Filming tips for perfect lip-sync
Copy
While Dubly is designed to be highly versatile and can successfully handle a wide variety of "edge cases," it is important to remember that the quality and complexity of your input significantly impact the final output. Great Lip-Sync starts on set. Follow this checklist when filming content destined for Dubly.AI.
Framing
Face fills 10–40 %
of the frame. Too small → less detail to animate; too close → any imperfection is magnified.
Front-facing or 3/4 profile.
Strict side profiles are much harder to re-animate.
Keep
head movement gentle
— no quick turns mid-sentence.
Lighting
Even light on the face
, no harsh shadows under the nose or around the mouth.
Avoid silhouettes
— backlight without fill lighting makes the mouth hard to track.
Match color temperature
across shots in the same scene so skin tones stay consistent.
Camera settings
Resolution
: 1080p minimum, 4K ideal.
Frame rate
: constant (CFR) 24, 25, or 30 fps. Avoid variable frame rate (VFR) recordings from some phones.
Shutter speed
: double the frame rate (e.g., 1/50 for 25 fps) to avoid blur.
Focus
: lock on the speaker's eyes so the mouth stays sharp.
Mouth visibility
No hands, props, or graphics
covering the lips during speech.
Speaker direction
Coach your speaker to:
Speak
clearly at a natural pace
— don't over-enunciate.
Pause between sentences
— gives Dubly.AI cleaner segment boundaries.
Limit side conversations and laughter
during takes.
Avoid chewing gum or snacking
on camera.
Audio
Lip-Sync still needs
clean source audio
to produce clean translations.
Post-production
Deliver
un-graded or lightly graded
footage if possible; heavy skin-smoothing can confuse face tracking.
Skip built-in subtitles
and lower-thirds over the mouth; export a clean version.
Export as
MP4 H.264 1080p, 48 kHz stereo
. A five-minute setup following this checklist saves hours of cleanup later.

title: Handle multiple speakers on camera
content:
Handle multiple speakers on camera
Copy
Videos with multiple people speaking — interviews, panels, podcasts, roundtables — need a bit more setup so each speaker gets the right dubbed voice. This article covers how Dubly handles multi-speaker content and what you need to do.
Before uploading: set the speaker count
In the dub creation modal there's a
Number of speakers
dropdown with three options:
1 speaker
— use this when only one person talks. Dubly skips speaker detection entirely.
2 speakers
— use this for two-person interviews and dialogues.
Auto-detect
— use this when there are 3 or more speakers in your video.
Why this matters:
if you set "1 speaker" on a video with two people, the second speaker's dialogue gets merged into the first speaker's voice — you lose the distinction. If you're in doubt, pick Auto-detect.
After transcription: assign voices
If your dub uses the
Replace Voice
cloning style and Dubly detects more than one speaker, your dub pauses in the
"Assign Voices"
state until you complete this step. You'll see a yellow warning badge on the dub in your list. Open the dub and you'll see a modal with one row per detected speaker:
Preview the original speaker
(if available) — click the play button next to the speaker label to hear their original voice.
Preview each preset voice
— click the play button next to a voice in the dropdown to hear what it sounds like.
Pick a voice
for each speaker. The Confirm button activates only when every speaker has a voice assigned.
Click
Confirm
— the dub resumes processing.
For
Original
and
Studio-Like
modes, Dubly clones each speaker's own voice automatically. No assignment step is needed, so the modal won't appear.
Where Dubly struggles with multi-speaker content
Even with Auto-detect, the model works best when speakers are clearly separated in the audio:
Distinct voices.
If speakers sound very similar, diarization may merge them into one.
Clean recording.
Heavy background music or echo makes it harder to isolate individual voices. If your audio has a lot of crosstalk, consider editing the source video first — cut the worst overlaps or re-record affected sections before uploading.
Can i change the speaker count?
There's no way to change it after the dub is created. Delete the dub and start over with the correct setting.
Still stuck?
If you've assigned all voices but the dub isn't resuming, or if the speaker count seems wrong after diarization, contact
support@dubly.ai
with the dub title and a short description of what you're seeing.

title: What is Custom Voice Clone?
content:
What is Custom Voice Clone?
Copy
A Custom Voice Clone is a saved voice you can reuse across multiple dubs — for example, your CEO's voice across every webinar, or a brand voice that should sound identical in every video you publish. It's different from the two voice options Dubly.AI uses by default:
Automatic cloning
of the speaker in your source video. This happens fresh on each dub and matches whoever is speaking on screen.
Studio voices
— high-quality, professionally trained voices you can pick from a list. Same voice, available to everyone. A Custom Voice Clone sits in between: a voice that's specifically yours, available across all your projects, that you don't have to recreate on every video.
When to use a Custom Voice Clone
You publish a series and want every episode to sound the same — even on segments where the source speaker is different.
You want to dub videos in a target language that the source speaker doesn't speak well enough to clone from.
You're producing voice-over-style content where the on-camera person isn't supposed to be the voice.
When automatic cloning is the better choice
One-off videos where matching the on-screen speaker is the goal.
Interviews, lectures, or any content where each speaker should keep their own voice.
Can I clone my own voice — or a colleague's?
Your own voice — yes, freely.
No additional permission needed; it's your voice.
A colleague's, employee's, or anyone else's voice — only with their explicit consent.
Specifically: written permission from the person whose voice will be cloned, naming Dubly.AI as the platform, naming you (or your company) as the user, and confirming the contexts in which the clone will be used. This isn't only a Dubly.AI policy — it's how voice rights actually work. Voice cloning without consent has clear legal exposure (depending on jurisdiction: rights of publicity, GDPR for EU subjects, defamation for misleading uses) and creates platform problems on YouTube and elsewhere. For details on the consent process, see
Handling Voice Cloning and Consent Requirements
.
What you cannot clone
Public figures, celebrities, or politicians
— without explicit signed consent, this is off-limits regardless of how good the source audio is.
A voice you sourced from social media or YouTube
without the speaker's consent.
A voice from a dataset or recording where consent isn't documented.
How to set up a Custom Voice Clone
Custom Voice Clone setup is currently handled together with our team. Contact support and tell them:
Whose voice it is (and that you have permission to use it).
Where the voice should be used (single project, all your projects).
Sample audio of the voice if you have it. We'll configure it for your account so you can pick it as a target voice on any future dub.

title: Preserve emotion and tone across languages
content:
Preserve emotion and tone across languages
Copy
A good dub doesn't just translate the words — it carries the speaker's energy, emphasis, and mood into the new language. This article explains how Dubly.AI transfers emotion and what you can do to get the best result.
How emotion transfer actually works
When you use
Original
or
Studio-Like
as your voice cloning style, Dubly.AI clones the speaker's voice from your source audio. The clone captures more than just timbre — it captures:
Pitch and melody
— how the voice rises and falls across a sentence.
Pace and rhythm
— where the speaker slows down, speeds up, or pauses.
Emphasis
— which syllables or words the speaker stresses.
Breathing and energy
— subtle cues that make a voice sound confident, tired, excited, or calm. When the translation is synthesized, these characteristics are applied to the new language. The dubbed line ends up with a similar emotional contour to the original — even though the words and the rhythm of the target language are different. There are
no manual emotion controls
(no "happy", "sad", "excited" tags). Emotion transfer is automatic and driven entirely by your source audio plus the voice model.
What you can do to help
1. Give the model clean source audio
Emotion is in the nuance — and nuance is the first thing that gets lost to bad audio. Upload source material with:
A clean voice track (lavalier or shotgun microphone, quiet room).
Background music at least 12 dB below the speaker during dialogue.
No heavy voice effects (deep reverb, telephone filter, auto-tune). If you have to choose between shorter-but-clean and longer-but-noisy, go clean. The clone sounds more expressive on 60 seconds of pristine audio than 10 minutes of background noise.
2. Punctuate the source text well
When the AI re-translates and re-synthesizes a sentence, it uses punctuation to decide rhythm:
Commas
create short breath breaks.
Periods
create full stops and reset the melody.
Question marks
lift the pitch at the end.
Exclamation marks
drive the energy up. If you find yourself fixing a flat-sounding dubbed line in the transcript editor, check the source text's punctuation first. A missing comma at the right spot often revives a whole line.
3. Use the tone field in your Translation Style
A Translation Style has a
Tone
field (professional, casual, energetic, etc.) and a
Formality
setting. These shape the translator's word choice — which in turn shapes what the voice model has to say. An energetic tone with vivid verbs gives the voice more to work with than a flat, literal translation. See
Maintaining Brand Language and Terminology Databases
for how to set this up.
4. Pick the right cloning mode
Original
preserves the full range of the speaker's emotion — including rough edges. Use when authenticity matters more than polish.
Studio-Like
smooths the voice into a cleaner sound. You keep the speaker's voice identity but lose a bit of the raw emotional texture. Attention: Do not use if you already have clear audio quality
Replace Voice
uses a library voice. Emotion comes from the library voice's natural delivery and the translated text's rhythm — the
original speaker's
emotion is not transferred, because their voice isn't used at all.
Where emotion transfer has limits
Even at its best, emotion transfer is a close approximation, not a perfect copy:
Whispered, shouted, or extreme voice states
can soften in the clone. If a scene depends on a whispered confession or a full shout, expect some loss.
Very fast speech
may feel slightly flatter in the target language — some languages need more syllables to say the same thing, and the model has to fit them into the original timing.
Emotional laughs, cries, and vocalizations
are not synthesized. These stay in the source audio only; the translated dialogue around them is dubbed.
Multi-speaker overlap
in the source can confuse the cloner — the resulting emotion may blend two voices. Record cleanly separated dialogue when you can.
Fine-tuning after the dub
If a specific sentence in the dub feels emotionally off:
Open the
Edit Translation
tab.
Find the sequence and play both the original and dubbed audio.
Adjust the
target text
— reword for punch, add a comma for breath, or split a run-on sentence into two. Save and let the audio re-synthesize. It's usually two or three sequences per dub that need a touch-up — not the whole video.
The short version
Dubly.AI transfers emotion automatically through voice cloning. Your job is to
give it good raw material
(clean audio, clear punctuation, a decent Translation Style) and
fix the outliers
in the transcript editor afterwards. You don't need to tag emotion manually — the voice does the work.

title: Lip-sync limitations and workarounds
content:
Lip-sync limitations and workarounds
Copy
Lip-Sync re-animates the speaker's mouth to match the dubbed audio — but it doesn't work on every shot and knowing its limits saves you credits and frustration. This article covers where Lip-Sync struggles and what you can do about it.
Where Lip-Sync struggles
Lip-Sync depends on the AI seeing a face clearly on screen. It tends to produce weak results in these situations:
The speaker isn't facing the camera
(profile shots, quick turns away). Face tracking can lose the mouth.
The face is small in the frame
(wide shots, crowd scenes, group photos). Detail is too low for realistic mouth movement.
The mouth is partially hidden
— microphones held close, hands in front of the face, hoodies, masks, heavy beards.
Very fast cuts or rapid head motion.
The model has less time to re-animate between frames.
What Dubly does automatically
You don't need to mark anything. The pipeline:
Detects faces per segment.
Segments with no face visible are passed through unchanged — Lip-Sync is applied only where a face is on screen.
Normalizes the video
to H.264 MP4 at up to 1920px width and 30 FPS before lip-syncing, so mild encoding quirks in the source don't break the pipeline.
Retries per-segment failures
up to five times before marking that segment failed. Other segments continue independently.
What you can do
Before you run Lip-Sync:
Crop tighter on the speaker's face
if it's small in the frame — a tighter composition gives the model more to work with.
Cut out non-speaking B-roll
— if your video has long stretches where nobody is talking on camera, Lip-Sync isn't adding value there anyway.
Avoid strong face occlusion
or extreme angles where possible.
When Lip-Sync fails completely
If Lip-Sync produces a "Failed" status on the dub page, the most common cause is
no face detected anywhere in the video
. Animation, motion graphics, product videos without presenters, or voice-over-only footage can't be lip-synced.
Cost reminder
Lip-Sync is billed separately from dubbing at
1 credit per minute
per subdub. If a shot clearly won't benefit from Lip-Sync (wide shots, no speaker on camera), skipping Lip-Sync for that language saves you that credit spend — see
When to Enable Lip-Sync
for the decision framework.
Still problems?
If your source video meets the quality bar above — clear mouth, good framing, stable lighting, consistent frame rate — and you're still facing problems or your lip-sync failed completely, contact our
support team
with:
The dub link
A timestamp of the problem segment
A short description of the problem
This helps us investigate whether it's something we need to tune on our side.

title: Pricing Model and Translation Costs
content:
Pricing Model and Translation Costs
Copy
Dubly.AI bills on a simple unit:
1 minute = 1 credit
. This article explains how the cost is calculated, when you're charged and what's included.
The formula
For each dub you create:
Cost (in credits) = video length in minutes × number of target languages
A few details:
Video length is
rounded up
to the next full minute. A 10-minute 5-second video counts as 11 minutes.
Each additional target language multiplies the cost. A 10-minute video into 3 languages =
30 credits
.
Every language costs the same.
There's no premium for rare languages.
Voice cloning
and
custom voices
don't add extra cost — they're included.
Example
You want to translate 10 videos of 15 minutes each into 2 languages. 10 × 15 × 2 =
300 credits
for the dubbing.
Lip-sync costs extra
Lip-sync is billed separately, at the same rate:
Lip-sync cost = video length in minutes (per language, per pass)
A 10-minute video that you dub into one language
and
lip-sync costs:
10 credits (dub) + 10 credits (lip-sync) = 20 credits
. You can lip-sync each target language independently — you're only charged when you actually run lip-sync on a given language.
See the cost before you commit
When you open the dub creation modal and pick your target languages, the footer shows a live
"Cost: X Minute(s)"
. It updates as you add or remove languages and warns you if you don't have enough credits. You're only charged when you click to create the dub — browsing the setup is free.
When you're charged
Credits are deducted
at the moment the dub is created
, before processing starts.
Failed dubs are not automatically refunded.
If a dub fails for a technical reason, email
hello@dubly.ai
with the dub in question and we'll review it.
Free credit on signup
New accounts get
1 free credit
at signup — enough to test a short video end-to-end before subscribing. Users invited into an existing organization don't get a separate free credit; they use the organization's shared balance.
Choosing a plan
Two plan types:
Monthly plan
— full flexibility, cancel any time.
Yearly plan
— lower per-minute rate in exchange for a 12-month commitment. Pick the plan whose monthly minutes roughly match your translation volume. If your usage grows, upgrade mid-cycle. Use the calculator on the
pricing page
to preview different scenarios.

title: Billing and pricing overview
content:
Billing and pricing overview
Copy
Standard Payment Methods
For our
Monthly
and
Yearly
subscription plans, we offer a streamlined checkout experience. To ensure the highest level of security, all transactions are processed through
Stripe
, a global leader in payment processing. We currently accept the following payment methods:
Credit & Debit Cards:
(Visa, Mastercard, American Express, etc.)
PayPal:
For a quick and easy checkout. All payments are encrypted and secure. You can update your payment information at any time through your
Account Settings
.
Enterprise & High-Volume Solutions
For organizations with specialized requirements, large-scale processing needs or custom workflows, we provide
tailored billing arrangements
. We understand that Enterprise clients often require more flexibility than a standard credit card payment allows. For these plans, we can support:
Invoicing & Bank Transfers:
(Wire, ACH, or SEPA)
Custom Billing Cycles:
Aligned with your company's fiscal year or budget.
Purchase Orders (PO):
Integration with your internal procurement systems.
Custom Quotes & Offers:
Individualized pricing packages tailored to your specific project volume and feature needs.
Interested in an Enterprise Plan?
Our sales team will work closely with you to design a payment solution that fits your business model. To discuss your specific needs, please
Contact our Sales Team
.

title: Change or upgrade your plan
content:
Change or upgrade your plan
Copy
Where to manage your plan
Open
My Plan
from the sidebar. You'll see your current plan, remaining minutes, and all the options described below. Any member of your organization can open this page and trigger plan changes. Plan changes apply to the whole organization, not to individual members.
Upgrading
Switching to a plan with more minutes (on the same interval) takes effect
immediately
:
A new billing cycle starts now and you're charged the full price of the new plan.
Your new monthly minutes unlock right away.
Any remaining minutes from your old plan carry over into your balance.
Downgrading
Switching to a plan with fewer minutes on the same interval takes effect at the
end of your current billing cycle
:
You keep your current minutes and features until the cycle ends.
No refund is issued for the unused portion of the higher plan.
Subscription minutes don't roll over — any unused subscription minutes expire at renewal as they always do.
Switching between monthly and yearly
The two directions behave differently:
Monthly → Yearly
takes effect
immediately
. A new billing cycle starts now, you're charged the full yearly price and yearly pricing applies from this point on. Your remaining minutes carry over.
Yearly → Monthly
is scheduled for the
end of your current yearly period
. You keep yearly pricing and minutes until then; the monthly plan begins on the next renewal date. You can cancel a scheduled yearly→monthly change before it takes effect. Yearly plans offer a lower per-minute rate in exchange for a 12-month term.
Cancelling
Click
Manage Subscription
on the plan page to open the Stripe customer portal, then cancel from there. Once cancelled:
Your subscription stays active until the end of the already-paid period.
Remaining subscription minutes are usable until that date.
You're not charged again. If you cancelled by mistake, a
Reactivate Subscription
button appears on the plan page before the period ends.
Enterprise and custom plans
For higher volumes or custom contract terms, contact
sales@dubly.ai
.
Questions about a specific charge?
See
Understand your invoice
for what's on each invoice, or email
hello@dubly.ai
with the invoice number.

title: Cancellation policy
content:
Cancellation policy
Copy
Cancellation Policy
At Dubly.AI, we believe in keeping our billing transparent and user-friendly. You have full control over your subscription and can make changes at any time.
How Cancellation Works
If you are on a
Monthly
or
Yearly
subscription plan, you can cancel your membership whenever you choose. To ensure you get the full value of your plan, the cancellation will take effect at the
end of your current billing cycle
.
What this means for you:
Continuous Access:
You will retain full access to all features and your account until the period you've already paid for concludes.
No Further Charges:
Once you cancel, no further payments will be processed for the following month or year.
Examples
Monthly Plans:
If you cancel halfway through your billing month, your access remains active for the remaining two weeks. You will not be charged for the next month.
Yearly Plans:
If you cancel a few months into your annual subscription, you will continue to have access for the remainder of the year.
How to Cancel
If you wish to cancel your subscription, our team is here to help you with the process. You can reach out to us through either of the following channels:
Plan Settings:
Click
Manage Subscription
to open the Stripe customer portal, where you can cancel.
Live Chat:
Send us a message directly through the
live chat bubble
in the bottom-right corner of your screen for the fastest response.
Email:
Contact us at
hello@dubly.ai
and we will process your request as soon as possible.
Please note:
To help us handle your cancellation quickly, please reach out using the email address associated with your Dubly.AI account. Once your request is processed, you will receive a confirmation email.

title: Understand your invoice
content:
Understand your invoice
Copy
Where to find your invoices
Open
My Plan
from the sidebar.
Open the
Invoices
tab. You'll see your most recent invoices (up to 24) with date, amount and payment status. Click an invoice to open the
PDF version.
What's on your invoice
You'll see:
Invoice number and issue date
— the unique reference for this charge.
Billing address and VAT ID
— as entered during checkout.
Plan or purchase name
— e.g.
Monthly Plan
,
Yearly Plan
Quantity
— the number of minutes included in the line item.
Amount
— before and after tax, with currency.
Tax (VAT / GST / sales tax)
— calculated automatically based on your billing country.
Proration
— if you upgraded mid-cycle, a prorated adjustment for the partial period is shown.
Total
— the amount actually charged.
Billing address, company name and VAT ID
Your billing details are collected by Stripe during your
first checkout
— the time you subscribed. Stripe stores them on your customer record and reuses them on every future invoice automatically. To change them — after a move, a company rename or because you missed a field the first time — contact
hello@dubly.ai
with:
Your account email
The new billing details (company name, address, VAT ID)
Whether you need a corrected copy of a past invoice We'll update your Stripe customer and re-issue affected invoices if needed.
Who receives invoice emails
Please note that we do not send invoices via email at this time. To view or download your billing statements, simply log in to your account and navigate to the
My Plan
.
Refunds and corrections
If a charge needs adjusting, we issue a
credit note
from Stripe rather than altering the original invoice. Credit notes appear alongside your regular invoices in the Invoices tab and, where relevant, automatically reduce the amount owed on the next invoice.
Questions about a specific charge?
Email
hello@dubly.ai
with the
invoice number
from the header.

title: Fix failed payments and billing errors
content:
Fix failed payments and billing errors
Copy
If a subscription charge or a one-time purchase fails, this article walks you through how to find out, what it means for your account and how to fix it.
How you find out about a failed payment
Dubly.AI does
not
lock your account when a payment fails — your dubs keep working for as long as you have credits. The signal shows up in one place:
Open
My Plan
from the sidebar.
Open the
Invoices
tab.
Any invoice that didn't go through appears with a
"Failed"
badge. Stripe (our payment processor) also sends an email to the address on your account with the decline reason. That email usually arrives within minutes of the failed charge.
The most common reasons a charge fails
Card expired.
The most frequent cause — especially at renewal time.
Insufficient funds
on the card.
3-D Secure / bank verification required
and not completed.
Issuing bank blocked the charge
as suspicious (common for first-time international charges).
Billing address or postal code mismatch
at the card level. Dubly only shows a generic "failed" status — the precise reason comes from Stripe's email or your bank.
How to fix it
On the
My Plan
page, click
Manage Subscription
. This opens Stripe's secure customer portal in a new tab.
In the portal,
update your payment method
— add a new card or fix the existing one.
Retry the failed invoice by paying it from the same portal. Most failures succeed immediately once the card is valid again. Once a retry succeeds, your account continues as normal — the next invoice reverts to the regular schedule.
What happens if the payment stays unpaid
Please ensure your payment details are kept up to date. If multiple payment attempts fail without being resolved, we may be forced to temporarily suspend your account and begin a formal dunning process. However, if you think a payment has been handled incorrectly or by mistake, we are here to help please reach out to us at
hello@dubly.ai
Updating billing details for future invoices
Company name, billing address, and VAT ID that appear on your invoices are stored on Stripe's customer record, captured during your first checkout. To change them, contact
hello@dubly.ai
with:
Your account email
The new details (company name, address, VAT ID)
Whether you need a corrected copy of a past invoice We'll update Stripe and re-issue affected invoices if needed.
Still stuck?
If the portal won't load, the retry keeps failing, or the charge went through but you still see "Failed" in Dubly, email
hello@dubly.ai
with the
invoice number
from the Invoices tab. We'll check it directly in Stripe and confirm what's happening.

title: Refunds and credit recovery
content:
Refunds and credit recovery
Copy
The short version
What happened
What we do
Dub failed for a technical reason on our side
Credits restored after manual review
Dub finished but you're unhappy with the quality
No automatic refund — see options below
You used lip-sync and don't like the result
No automatic refund
You cancelled mid-cycle
Access continues until end of cycle, no money refunded
You downgraded mid-cycle
New plan applies next cycle, no money refunded
One-time credits you bought are unused
Generally non-refundable
Subscription was charged in error
Refundable if technical error. Forgot cancellation is NO reason
When credits are restored
We restore credits when a dub failed for a reason on our side. That includes:
A pipeline error during transcription, translation, voice synthesis, or lip-sync.
A platform incident. What's
not
covered:
The source video had no audio track, was silent, was corrupted or was in an unsupported format. Prevention: see
Do's and don'ts for your source video
and
Supported video
and audio formats
.
The translation reads correctly but you'd phrase it differently. Solution: edit translations before regeneration — see
Improve your translation quality
.
Lip-sync ran successfully but you don't like the visual result.
How to request credit recovery
Email
hello@dubly.ai
with:
The
dub title
(or a link to the dub).
A short description of
what failed
— error message, missing output, incorrect language, etc.
The
target language(s)
affected.
For lip-sync issues: a screenshot or screen recording showing the problem. We aim to respond within one business day. If we agree it's a technical failure on our side, the credits go back to your balance immediately.
Subscription refunds
Mid-cycle cancellation:
You keep access to your plan and remaining minutes until the end of the current billing period. The amount you've already paid is not refunded — see
Cancellation policy
.
Mid-cycle downgrade:
Your current plan stays active until the end of the cycle. The lower plan kicks in at the next renewal. The difference between plans is not refunded — see
Change or upgrade your plan
.
Mid-cycle upgrade:
The new plan takes effect immediately. You will be charged the
full amount
of the new plan upfront to give you instant access to all included credits. The unused portion of your previous plan is not refunded separately, but factored into the transition.
Charged in error:
If you were billed by mistake — duplicate charge, charge after cancellation, charge to the wrong card — contact us and we'll refund through your original payment method. EU customers retain all rights under consumer-protection law regardless of these defaults.
Lip-sync specifically
Lip-sync credits aren't refunded automatically if you decide not to use the result. The reason: every lip-sync run consumes external AI capacity that we pay for whether you keep the output or not. We build in a
first lip-sync run per dub free
so you can test before committing on longer videos. Use it. See
When should you turn on lip-sync?
and
Lip-sync limitations and workarounds
.
Enterprise contracts
Enterprise agreements may override these defaults — refund terms, SLAs, and credit-recovery clauses are negotiated case by case. Check your MSA or contact your account manager.
See also
Cancellation policy
Change or upgrade your plan
Billing and pricing overview
How much does a translation cost?
Fix failed payments and billing errors

title: Can I pay for credits on demand?
content:
Can I pay for credits on demand?
Copy
The default: subscriptions
To dub videos in Dubly.AI you need an active plan. That subscription gives you a monthly or yearly allocation of minutes that resets on each billing renewal. If you don't have a subscription, you can't buy credits — there's no checkout option for "I'd like to buy 50 minutes" without a subscription attached.
Looking to avoid recurring monthly payments?
If a monthly subscription doesn't fit your needs, our
Yearly Plan
is an excellent alternative. It allows you to access a larger credit balance (e.g., 300 credits) upfront, which is available for the full 12-month period. To prevent any future charges, you can simply cancel the plan immediately after purchase; you will still retain full access to all your credits until the end of the term without any automatic renewal.
What if a subscription doesn't fit my situation?
If our standard plans don't quite fit your workflow, we're happy to design a custom solution tailored to your specific volume. Feel free to reach out to us at
sales@dubly.ai
or
book a quick call
to discuss your needs:
https://go.dubly.ai/book

title: Why did my plan automatically renew?
content:
Why did my plan automatically renew?
Copy
Subscriptions on Dubly.AI auto-renew at the end of every billing cycle. If you don't cancel, you'll be charged for the next cycle on your renewal date.
When the renewal happens
Your renewal date is shown on the plan page. For monthly plans, it's the same calendar day every month (e.g. the 14th). For yearly plans, it's the same date every year. We charge the payment method on file. If the card is expired or declined, the renewal fails and we'll notify you to update your payment method.
How to stop auto-renewal
Go to My plan and click
Cancel subscription
. You'll get a confirmation prompt. After cancelling:
You keep access through the end of the cycle you've already paid for.
No charge happens at the next renewal date.
Your subscription minutes stop refilling at the next renewal date.
Your dubs, projects and Account members all stay.
"I cancelled but I still got charged"
Two common reasons:
You cancelled too late in the cycle.
Cancellation only stops the
next
renewal. If your renewal was today and you cancelled today, the renewal may have already gone through.
You only cancelled in your bank app.
Telling your bank to "block Dubly.AI" doesn't cancel your subscription on our side — it just causes the next charge to fail. You're still on the subscription. Cancel through the plan page so the cancellation is on Dubly.AI's records. If you cancelled in the plan page and were still charged, contact support with the date of cancellation.
Refund eligibility
See
Refunds and credit recovery
for what we can and can't refund.

title: Information about credit usage
content:
Information about credit usage
Copy
Open
plan
from the navigation. You'll see two main areas:
Plan Card
The card at the top shows your current plan — name, monthly minute allocation, and your renewal date. If you've cancelled, it shows when access expires. If you're past due on payment, it shows that here too.
Transactions tab
This is the meat of the page. It lists every credit movement on your account in chronological order:
Type
— subscription refill, dub consumption, refund, or admin adjustment.
Minutes
— how many were added or used.
Source
— whether subscription or OTP credits were affected.
Balance before / balance after
— the running totals so you can see how a transaction changed your standing.
Timestamp
— when the transaction happened.
What's not on this page
Per-dub minute breakdown across all your dubs
— for that, open individual dubs.
Export-to-CSV
— see
Can I export usage data for my account?
.

title: Can I export usage data for my account?
content:
Can I export usage data for my account?
Copy
What you can see in the UI
Open your plan page and switch to the Transactions tab. You'll see:
Every credit movement (subscription refills, OTP purchases, dubs that consumed credits)
The minute amount and source for each entry
Your balance before and after each transaction
Date and time of every transaction
Your invoices, with PDF download via Stripe For most workflows, scrolling and filtering the Transactions tab is enough.
What we hear most often
The single most common ask is "monthly minutes used in Q1". If that's all you need, the Transactions tab plus a calculator is faster than a support ticket — and your invoices already give you the financial side of the same story.

title: Do you offer any discount?
content:
Do you offer any discount?
Copy
We don't run public discount codes, sales or promo campaigns. Pricing is the same for everyone on the standard plans. That said, custom pricing exists for the right cases — if you fit one, talk to us.
When custom pricing is on the table
High-volume usage
— if your monthly dubbing volume sits well above what our standard tiers cover, we can structure a custom plan that's better than just stacking the highest tier.
Multi-account / team setups
— if you're running several Dubly.AI accounts for different brands or clients, we can talk about a consolidated arrangement.
Long-term commitments
— annual or multi-year contracts can move the needle on price.
Specific use cases
— agencies, enterprise customers with procurement processes, or partners building Dubly.AI into their own workflows.
How to ask
Email support with a short summary:
Your expected monthly volume (in minutes)
Your use case in one sentence
Whether you're open to an annual contract
Feel free to reach out to us at
sales@dubly.ai
or
book a quick call
to discuss your needs:
https://go.dubly.ai/book
What you can do today, without a custom plan
Switch from monthly to yearly billing
— yearly plans are priced lower per minute than monthly.

title: Can I pause my subscription?
content:
Can I pause my subscription?
Copy
Most "pause my subscription" requests come from one of three places:
You're going on vacation or won't dub for a few months
— you don't want to pay during downtime.
You overestimated your usage
and you'd rather a temporary break than a full downgrade.
You're between projects
and unsure whether you'll keep using Dubly.AI at all. For all three, the workaround is the same.
The workaround
Cancel
at the end of your current billing cycle. You keep access (and your remaining subscription minutes) until the end of the period you've already paid for.
Re-subscribe
when you need it again. Your account stays put — login, dubs, projects, custom voices, glossaries, settings — all of it is preserved.
What you don't lose
Your dubs, projects, custom voices, glossaries, and Translation Styles all stay in your account.
Your team members stay in the Account.
Your past invoices stay accessible.

title: When do my credits reset?
content:
When do my credits reset?
Copy
